title: The Turing Test
description: Alan Turing proposes the Turing Test as a criterion for machine intelligence, asking whether a machine can imitate human responses well enough to be indistinguishable from a person in conversation.

title: Al-Jazari's Automata
description: Al-Jazari designs programmable automata, early examples of mechanical devices that could perform tasks automatically, representing a precursor to modern robotics and AI.

title: Ramon Llull's Logical Machines
description: Ramon Llull develops logical machines to combine basic truths mechanically, influencing later ideas of mechanized reasoning.

title: Pascal's Calculator
description: Blaise Pascal invents the mechanical calculator, an early step toward automated computation.

title: Leibniz's Stepped Reckoner and Calculus Ratiocinator
description: Gottfried Wilhelm Leibniz builds the Stepped Reckoner and envisions the Calculus Ratiocinator, a universal logical calculation engine.

title: Babbage's Analytical Engine
description: Charles Babbage designs the Analytical Engine, the first concept of a programmable computer.

title: Ada Lovelace's Notes on the Analytical Engine
description: Ada Lovelace speculates on the Analytical Engine's ability to manipulate symbols and compose music, foreseeing general-purpose computation.

title: Cybernetics Published
description: Norbert Wiener publishes 'Cybernetics', founding the study of control and communication in animals and machines, influencing early AI.

title: Turing Machine Proposed
description: Alan Turing introduces the concept of the Turing machine, a theoretical model that formalizes the idea of computation and underpins computer science and AI.

title: First Artificial Neuron Model
description: Warren McCulloch and Walter Pitts publish a paper describing the first mathematical model of an artificial neuron, laying the foundation for neural networks.

title: SNARC: First Neural Net Machine
description: Marvin Minsky and Dean Edmonds build SNARC, the first artificial neural network machine, pioneering hardware-based AI research.

title: Logic Theorist
description: Allen Newell and Herbert A. Simon develop the Logic Theorist, the first program to mimic human problem-solving by proving mathematical theorems.

title: General Problem Solver
description: Newell and Simon create the General Problem Solver, a universal problem-solving machine that influenced AI planning and search algorithms.

title: Samuel's Checkers Program
description: Arthur Samuel develops a checkers-playing program that learns from experience, an early example of machine learning.

title: Dartmouth Workshop
description: The Dartmouth Summer Research Project on Artificial Intelligence, led by John McCarthy, Marvin Minsky, Nathaniel Rochester, and Claude Shannon, is held. This event is considered the birth of AI as a field.

title: The Perceptron
description: Frank Rosenblatt invents the perceptron, an early neural network model capable of learning simple patterns, laying the groundwork for modern neural networks.

title: ADALINE and MADALINE Neural Networks
description: Bernard Widrow and Ted Hoff develop ADALINE and MADALINE, early neural networks used for practical applications.

title: ELIZA: First Chatbot
description: Joseph Weizenbaum creates ELIZA, an early natural language processing computer program that simulates conversation, laying the foundation for future chatbots and conversational AI.

title: Shakey the Robot
description: SRI International develops Shakey, the first mobile robot able to reason about its actions, integrating perception, planning, and action.

title: STRIPS Planning System
description: The Stanford Research Institute Problem Solver (STRIPS) is introduced, becoming a foundational algorithm for automated planning.

title: PARRY Chatbot
description: Kenneth Colby creates PARRY, a chatbot simulating paranoid schizophrenia, advancing conversational AI.

title: MYCIN Expert System
description: MYCIN, an early expert system for medical diagnosis, demonstrates the power of rule-based AI in healthcare.

title: Actor Model Introduced
description: Carl Hewitt introduces the Actor model, a conceptual foundation for concurrent computation and distributed AI systems.

title: Conceptual Dependency Theory
description: Roger Schank develops Conceptual Dependency Theory, a framework for representing natural language meaning in AI.

title: Frames Introduced
description: Marvin Minsky introduces frames, a data structure for representing stereotyped situations, influencing knowledge representation in AI.

title: Expert Systems Era
description: Rule-based expert systems like MYCIN and XCON are developed, enabling computers to perform tasks that previously required human expertise in fields such as medicine and business.

title: ALPAC Report
description: The US government publishes the ALPAC report, criticizing the lack of progress in machine translation and leading to a major reduction in AI funding.

title: Perceptrons Book Published
description: Marvin Minsky and Seymour Papert publish 'Perceptrons', highlighting limitations of single-layer neural networks and contributing to the first AI winter.

title: Lighthill Report
description: The Lighthill report in the UK criticizes AI research, leading to reduced funding and the first AI winter in Britain.

title: Moravec's Paradox
description: Hans Moravec articulates the paradox that high-level reasoning requires little computation, but low-level sensorimotor skills require enormous computational resources.

title: Dreyfus Critique: What Computers Can't Do
description: Hubert Dreyfus publishes 'What Computers Can't Do', critiquing the assumptions of symbolic AI and emphasizing the limits of computation.

title: Backpropagation Popularized
description: David Rumelhart, Geoffrey Hinton, and Ronald Williams publish the backpropagation algorithm, enabling the training of multi-layer neural networks and laying the foundation for deep learning.

title: Hopfield Network Introduced
description: John Hopfield introduces the Hopfield network, a form of recurrent neural network that advances the field of connectionism.

title: Cyc Project Launched
description: Douglas Lenat launches the Cyc project, an ambitious effort to encode common sense knowledge for AI systems.

title: Weizenbaum's Computer Power and Human Reason
description: Joseph Weizenbaum publishes 'Computer Power and Human Reason', warning about the ethical and societal risks of AI.

title: Boltzmann Machine Introduced
description: Geoffrey Hinton and Terry Sejnowski introduce the Boltzmann machine, a stochastic recurrent neural network.

title: Bayesian Networks Popularized
description: Judea Pearl publishes 'Probabilistic Reasoning in Intelligent Systems', popularizing Bayesian networks for AI.

title: Searle's Chinese Room Argument
description: John Searle presents the Chinese Room argument, challenging the notion that computers can possess understanding or consciousness.

title: Fifth Generation Computer Project
description: Japan launches the Fifth Generation Computer Project, aiming to create computers using massively parallel computing and logic programming, influencing global AI research.

title: Deep Blue Defeats Kasparov
description: IBM’s Deep Blue defeats world chess champion Garry Kasparov, marking a major milestone in AI’s ability to solve complex, strategic problems.

title: AI Winter Begins
description: Collapse of the Lisp machine market and overhyped expert systems leads to the second AI winter, with widespread funding cuts and company failures.

title: Convolutional Neural Networks Applied
description: Yann LeCun applies convolutional neural networks to handwritten digit recognition, leading to practical applications in document processing.

title: Intelligent Agents Paradigm
description: The intelligent agent paradigm becomes widely accepted, defining AI as the study of systems that perceive and act in environments.

title: DARPA Grand Challenge
description: Stanford's autonomous vehicle wins the DARPA Grand Challenge, marking a breakthrough in self-driving car technology.

title: ImageNet Launched
description: Fei-Fei Li and colleagues launch ImageNet, a large-scale image database that becomes a key resource for training deep learning models.

title: Word2Vec Released
description: Google releases Word2Vec, a neural network-based word embedding model that advances natural language understanding and enables large language models.

title: Deep Learning Renaissance
description: Geoffrey Hinton and colleagues revive interest in deep neural networks by demonstrating effective training of deep belief networks, sparking the modern deep learning revolution.

title: IBM Watson Wins Jeopardy!
description: IBM’s Watson defeats human champions on the quiz show Jeopardy!, showcasing advances in natural language processing and question answering.

title: AlexNet Wins ImageNet
description: AlexNet, a deep convolutional neural network by Krizhevsky, Sutskever, and Hinton, wins the ImageNet competition by a large margin, demonstrating the power of deep learning for computer vision.

title: AlphaGo Defeats Lee Sedol
description: DeepMind’s AlphaGo defeats world champion Go player Lee Sedol, a feat previously thought to be decades away, demonstrating the power of reinforcement learning and deep neural networks.

title: Attention Is All You Need
description: 'Attention Is All You Need' by Vaswani et al. introduces the Transformer architecture, a novel neural network design based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely. This paper revolutionized natural language processing and deep learning.

title: AlphaFold Solves Protein Folding
description: DeepMind's AlphaFold achieves a breakthrough in protein structure prediction, solving a 50-year-old grand challenge in biology.

title: GPT-3 Released
description: OpenAI releases GPT-3, a large language model that demonstrates unprecedented capabilities in text generation and understanding.

title: 15.ai Voice Synthesis Launched
description: 15.ai, a free web application, demonstrates high-quality AI voice cloning for creative content, popularizing AI voice synthesis.

title: Anthropic Claude Launched
description: Anthropic releases Claude, a family of large language models, contributing to the generative AI boom.

title: Google Gemini Launched
description: Google launches Gemini (formerly Bard), a major competitor in the large language model space.

title: Gato Released by DeepMind
description: DeepMind releases Gato, a generalist agent capable of performing hundreds of tasks, advancing the field of foundation models.

title: Claude 3.5 Sonnet Released
description: Anthropic releases Claude 3.5 Sonnet, demonstrating improved performance in coding, workflows, and image analysis.

title: 2024 Nobel Prizes for AI
description: John Hopfield, Geoffrey Hinton, Demis Hassabis, John Jumper, and David Baker receive Nobel Prizes for their contributions to AI and protein folding.

title: ChatGPT Launched
description: OpenAI launches ChatGPT, a conversational AI based on GPT-3.5, making advanced language models widely accessible to the public and sparking global interest in generative AI.

title: Pause Giant AI Experiments Open Letter
description: Thousands of AI researchers and tech leaders sign an open letter calling for a pause on training AI systems more powerful than GPT-4, citing societal risks.

title: OpenClaw Released
description: Peter Steinberger releases OpenClaw (originally Clawdbot, later Moltbot), an open-source autonomous AI agent designed for agentic workflows across messaging platforms. OpenClaw quickly gains global adoption and becomes a symbol of the shift toward autonomous AI systems, but also raises new security and privacy concerns.

title: GPT-1 Released
description: OpenAI releases Generative Pre-trained Transformer (GPT-1), the first large-scale transformer-based language model, demonstrating the power of unsupervised pre-training for NLP tasks.

title: GPT-2 Released
description: OpenAI releases GPT-2, a much larger language model capable of generating coherent paragraphs of text, sparking debate about the risks of powerful generative models.

title: DALL-E 2 Released
description: OpenAI releases DALL-E 2, an AI system that can create realistic images and art from natural language descriptions, advancing text-to-image generation.

title: DALL-E Released
description: OpenAI introduces DALL-E, a neural network that creates images from textual descriptions, pioneering the field of text-to-image synthesis.

title: DALL-E 3 Released
description: OpenAI releases DALL-E 3, further improving the quality and controllability of text-to-image generation.

title: Codex Released
description: OpenAI releases Codex, a model capable of translating natural language into code, powering GitHub Copilot and advancing AI-assisted programming.

title: Whisper Released
description: OpenAI releases Whisper, a general-purpose speech recognition model trained on a large amount of multilingual and multitask supervised data.

title: GPT-4 Released
description: OpenAI releases GPT-4, a large multimodal model that accepts image and text inputs, achieving state-of-the-art results on a range of professional and academic benchmarks.

title: GPT-3.5 Released
description: OpenAI releases GPT-3.5, an improved version of GPT-3 that powers the initial release of ChatGPT and demonstrates enhanced reasoning and coding abilities.

title: Sora Announced
description: OpenAI announces Sora, a text-to-video model capable of generating high-fidelity videos from textual prompts, marking a major advance in generative AI for video.

title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Introduced
description: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is introduced as an open standard for interoperable AI agent communication, enabling agents and tools to share context, goals, and results across platforms. MCP aims to standardize how AI models, agents, and applications interact, supporting the rise of agentic workflows and multi-agent systems.

title: Anthropic Launches Claude Agent Skills
description: Anthropic introduces Agent Skills for Claude, enabling users to automate expertise and workflows. Skills can be stacked for complex tasks and help capture institutional knowledge.

title: Andrej Karpathy's Vibe Coding Tweet
description: Andrej Karpathy tweets about 'vibe coding', describing the feeling of effortless, creative programming when everything flows smoothly.
